narrative: At 2026-05-16T06:43:00Z, service_account_creation on IAM was changed from approval_required to self_service by autoscaler-staging via a ci_cd change. Approval status was 'pending' and the change occurred outside an approved maintenance window. This maps to AC-2 (Account Management) and MITRE technique T1136 (Create Account), representing a CRITICAL severity finding.
remediation_steps: Revoke or scope down the affected role/trust relationship immediately.
Review recent activity performed under the affected account or role.
Confirm the change had a valid approval; escalate if none exists.

narrative: At 2026-07-05T02:27:00Z, encryption_algorithm on AWS RDS was changed from AES-256 to AES-128 by ci-cd-pipeline-01 via a ci_cd change. Approval status was 'not_required' and the change occurred outside an approved maintenance window. This maps to SC-13 (Cryptographic Protection) and MITRE technique T1600 (Weaken Encryption), representing a HIGH severity finding.
remediation_steps: Roll the encryption setting back to the approved baseline.
Re-encrypt affected data under the correct algorithm if a downgrade window existed.
Confirm no data was written or transmitted while the weaker setting was active.

narrative: At 2026-06-04T13:53:00Z, heartbeat_interval on Availability Monitor was changed from 60 to 900 by terraform-apply-bot via a iac change. Approval status was 'not_required' and the change occurred outside an approved maintenance window. This maps to SI-4 (System Monitoring) and MITRE technique T1499 (Endpoint Denial of Service), representing a CRITICAL severity finding.
remediation_steps: Restart or reinstall the affected agent and confirm active heartbeat.
Check for a bulk pattern across other hosts to rule out a coordinated disable.
Cross-check patch/deployment logs for a conflicting update as the root cause.

narrative: At 2026-05-20T15:06:00Z, alert_review_frequency on SIEM was changed from daily to weekly by admin_019 via a manual change. Approval status was 'pending' and the change occurred outside an approved maintenance window. This maps to AU-6 (Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting) and MITRE technique T1654 (Log Enumeration), representing a HIGH severity finding.
remediation_steps: Re-enable logging immediately and verify log delivery to the SIEM.
Audit the change history for this control to identify how long the gap lasted.
File an incident ticket if the gap exceeds the org's acceptable blind-spot window.

narrative: At 2026-04-17T09:28:00Z, external_exposure on Admin Console was changed from False to True by terraform-apply-bot via a iac change. Approval status was 'pending' and the change occurred outside an approved maintenance window. This maps to SC-7 (Boundary Protection) and MITRE technique T1686 (Disable or Modify System Firewall), representing a HIGH severity finding.
remediation_steps: Close or scope the exposed port/rule to the minimum required source range.
Verify no unauthorized connections occurred while the rule was broadened.
Add the change to change-management if it is a legitimate but undocumented need.

blast_radius: EC2 -> IAM role -> S3 bucket (payment records) -- reachable once logging, the admin console, and encryption are all weakened simultaneously
narrative: Between 2026-04-08T02:47:00Z and 2026-04-08T03:22:00Z, three controls were weakened by the same actor (admin_007): CloudTrail logging was disabled, an admin console was externally exposed, and database encryption was downgraded from AES-256 to AES-128. Individually these might be routine changes; together they open a path from the exposed console to encrypted payment data with no audit trail. This aligns with MITRE techniques T1685.002, T1686, T1600 and represents a PCI-DSS and GDPR Article 32 compliance failure.
remediation_sequence: 1. Re-enable CloudTrail logging first to restore audit visibility before touching anything else.
2. Close external exposure on the admin console to cut off the active access path.
3. Roll encryption back to AES-256 and verify no data was written under the weaker cipher.
4. Review all actions taken by the responsible actor during the 35-minute window.
